one_time_description: Impairment loss on tangible capital assets (assets under construction disposed)
economic_notes: COVID-19 impacted operations through social distancing, reduced gaming revenues, and reduced office hours. ISC increased funding to ensure safe provision of services. Major capital projects underway: new band office and daycare ($5,996,000), water treatment plant well upgrade ($6,500,000). Saskatchewan River Business Corporation (SRBC) and Cumberland House Saskatchewan River LP (CHSRLP) are business entities accounted for by modified equity method.
notes: Fiscal year is 2021, not 2024. Province is Saskatchewan. The nation has two GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: Saskatchewan River Business Corporation (SRBC) and Cumberland House Saskatchewan River LP (CHSRLP). One consolidated subsidiary: Saskatchewan River Community Development Corporation. CMHC Section 95 Housing Program also consolidated. Restricted cash totals $3,179,379 ($665,709 capital projects + $2,513,670 CMHC reserves). Bank indebtedness of $150,692 used instead of positive cash balance. Promissory notes of $2,210,971 represent non-interest bearing advances from Government of Canada to fund specific and agricultural claims. Large increase in long-term debt from $8,779,411 to $11,332,825 due to new FNB loans for legal claims and new band office construction. Prior year had large insurance settlement of $1,688,936. No schedule of remuneration provided in the document. Annual debt service of $4,797,390 represents repayments per cash flow statement.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
